Taishu-kai.description: The Taishu-kai is a yakuza organization based in Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan, with an estimated 60 active members. It was formed around 1954 by Kuniharu Ota as the Ota Group, later renamed Ota-gumi, and finally Taishu-kai in 1973. It is one of five independent Fukuoka-based designated yakuza syndicates and a member of the anti-Yamaguchi-gumi Yonsha-kai federation.

descriptions: Dojin-kai and Taishu-kai are allies per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Kudo-kai, Sinaloa cartel, Taishu-kai, Kumamoto-kai)

descriptions: Kudo-kai and Taishu-kai are both members of the Yonsha-kai federation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kudo-kai is a member of an anti-Yamaguchi fraternal federation, the Yonsha-kai, with three other northern-Kyushu based organizations, the Taishu-kai , Dojin-kai , and Kumamoto-kai.

descriptions: Taishu-kai and Dojin-kai are both members of the Yonsha-kai federation, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Taishu-kai is a member of an anti-Yamaguchi-gumi fraternal federation, the "Yonsha-kai", along with the Kitakyushu -based Kudo-kai , the Kurume -based Dojin-kai and the Kumamoto -based Kumamoto-kai.

descriptions: Taishu-kai and Kudo-kai are both members of the Yonsha-kai federation, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Taishu-kai is a member of an anti-Yamaguchi-gumi fraternal federation, the "Yonsha-kai", along with the Kitakyushu -based Kudo-kai , the Kurume -based Dojin-kai and the Kumamoto -based Kumamoto-kai.

descriptions: Taishu-kai and Kumamoto-kai are both members of the Yonsha-kai federation, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Taishu-kai is a member of an anti-Yamaguchi-gumi fraternal federation, the "Yonsha-kai", along with the Kitakyushu -based Kudo-kai , the Kurume -based Dojin-kai and the Kumamoto -based Kumamoto-kai.

descriptions: Taishu-kai is a designated yakuza group, a subset of Yakuza.
edges.evidence_quote: Fukuoka Prefecture has the largest number of designated yakuza groups among all of the prefectures, at 5; the Kudo-kai , the Taishu-kai , the Fukuhaku-kai , the Dojin-kai , and the Namikawa-kai .

descriptions: Taishu-kai is part of a federation that opposes Yamaguchi-gumi, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The Taishu-kai is a member of an anti-Yamaguchi-gumi fraternal federation, the "Yonsha-kai"

descriptions: The Barbaro-Papalia, Mancuso, and Ursino-Macrì were involved in drug trafficking together.
edges.evidence_quote: ha portato all'arresto per traffico di droga di 13 persone, presunte affiliate ai Mancuso , Barbaro- Papalia e agli Ursino - Macrì .

descriptions: Belfiore forms alliances with Ursino-Macrì in Turin.
edges.evidence_quote: A Torino stringono alleanze con gli Ursino -Macrì.

descriptions: Belfiore and Ursino jointly ordered the murder of magistrate Bruno Caccia.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 26 giugno 1983 fanno uccidere insieme agli Ursino a Torino il magistrato Bruno Caccia.

descriptions: Members of Ursino-Macrì and Ierinò were arrested together, indicating association.

descriptions: Mancuso and Ursino-Macrì were targeted together in operation Tamburo, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: operazione "Tamburo" ... ha portato all'arresto ... presunte affiliate ai Mancuso, Barbaro - Papalia e agli Ursino - Macrì

descriptions: Papalia and Ursino-Macrì were both targeted in the same drug trafficking operation.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 14 gennaio 2014 si conclude a Milano, l'operazione "Tamburo", iniziata nel 2009 che ha portato all'arresto per traffico di droga di 13 persone, presunte affiliate ai Mancuso , Barbaro-Papalia e agli Ursino - Macrì .

descriptions: Ursino-Macrì allied with Sgambellone for drug trafficking; Commisso is part of Ursino-Macrì.
edges.evidence_quote: si alleano con la 'ndrina (cosca) Sgambellone che gestivano un traffico di droga internazionale e nazionale per le Marche e l' Emilia-Romagna.

descriptions: Barahama Alih Group is alleged to be responsible for kidnappings, but no direct relationship with Abu Sayyaf is stated; they are separate groups.
edges.evidence_quote: Military sources allege Barahama Alih Group was responsible for a number of kidnapping incidents in previous years including the abduction of Australian national Warren Rodwell, Italian priest Giancarlo Bossi, and some local Filipinos.

descriptions: The Gramuglia were part of the Gallico faction in the feud.

descriptions: Sendero Rojo was a faction that split from Shining Path after rejecting Guzmán's peace treaty.
edges.evidence_quote: the Militarized Communist Party of Peru (MPCP) is considered the group's main successor, founded in 1999 by brothers Víctor and Jorge Quispe Palomino after the collapse of Sendero Rojo , the faction that had rejected Guzmán's peace treaty.

descriptions: Sendero Rojo is a splinter faction of Shining Path.
edges.evidence_quote: Guzmán's role as the leader of the Shining Path was taken over by Óscar Ramírez ("Comrade Feliciano"), who established Sendero Rojo (or PCP Pro-Seguir), aiming to reorganise the party and to continue the armed struggle while breaking with Guzmán, but not with his ideology.

Five Families.description: The Five Families are the five Italian American Mafia crime families operating in New York City, organized in 1931 by Salvatore Maranzano after the Castellammarese War. They are the Bonanno, Colombo, Gambino, Genovese, and Lucchese families, each with demarcated territory and hierarchy, reporting to the Commission. They have been publicly known since the Valachi hearings in 1963.

descriptions: The Caligiuri have connections with the Colombo family in Brooklyn.
edges.evidence_quote: con collegamenti nel Lazio in Lombardia , Germania e Stati Uniti (in particolare a New York , nel quartiere di Brooklyn ) con le famiglie Colombo e Gambino .

descriptions: The Bonanno family is one of the Five Families.
edges.evidence_quote: The Five Families are the five Italian American Mafia crime families who operate in New York City . In 1931, the Five Families were organized by Salvatore Maranzano following his victory in the Castellammarese War . Maranzano reorganized the Italian American gangs in New York City into the Maranzano, Profaci, Mangano, Luciano, and Gagliano families, which are now known as the Bonanno , Colombo , Gambino , Genovese , and Lucchese families, respectively.

descriptions: The Buffalo crime family was part of the Commission alongside the Five Families.
edges.evidence_quote: It consisted of the bosses of the Five Families as well as the bosses of the Chicago Outfit and the Buffalo crime family .

110ers.description: Criminal organization based in New Orleans, Louisiana, operating in the 10th and 11th Wards of the Uptown area. Primarily African American, involved in drug trafficking, murder, theft, and kidnapping. Known for violent feuds with rival gangs such as Young Melph Mafia and Ride or Die Gang. In 2013, 15 members were indicted in a major federal case linked to multiple killings.

descriptions: The 39ers gang is listed as a rival of the 110ers Gang in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Ride or Die Gang , Young Melph Mafia , 39ers gang

descriptions: The 110ers Gang feuds with the Ride or Die Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: In recent years the gang have been known to feud with the Young Melph Mafia and the Ride or Die Gang from the 8th Ward of New Orleans.

descriptions: The 110ers Gang feuds with the Young Melph Mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: In recent years the gang have been known to feud with the Young Melph Mafia and the Ride or Die Gang from the 8th Ward of New Orleans.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: 110ers

Fuerza Anti-Unión.description: Mexican criminal organization based in Tepito, Mexico City, founded around 2016. It emerged possibly as a self-defense group against Unión Tepito or as a splinter faction. Engages in extortion, kidnapping, drug trafficking, and murder. Despite arrests of leaders, the group remains active.

edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: CJNG , Fuerza Anti-Unión , La Familia Michoacana , Células delictivas locales y de menor importancia como La Ronda 88 y Los Guerreros

'Ndrina Comberiati.description: The Comberiati are a 'ndrina (organized crime clan) of the 'Ndrangheta, originating from Petilia Policastro, Italy. They are allied with the Coco-Trovato clan and have controlled neighborhoods in Milan, particularly Quarto Oggiaro. The clan was involved in the murder of witness Lea Garofalo in 2009. Key members include boss Vincenzo Comberiati, arrested and sentenced to 41-bis solitary confinement.

descriptions: Comberiati together with Carvelli controlled neighborhoods in Milan.
edges.evidence_quote: insieme alla famiglia Carvelli avrebbero controllato alcuni quartieri di Milano

descriptions: Comberiati are allies of Coco-Trovato.
edges.evidence_quote: Sarebbero alleati dei Coco-Trovato

descriptions: The text contrasts the Continentals with White Eagles, noting that White Eagles recruited immigrants while Continentals could not.
edges.evidence_quote: After some time they had trouble recruiting new members due to the small number of American-born Chinese, whereas new gangs such as the White Eagles and Chung Ching Yee in California were able to recruit new members who were immigrants from Hong Kong and mainland China.

descriptions: The text contrasts the SDG with the White Eagles, noting differences in nationalist sincerity.
edges.evidence_quote: In this, the SDG differed significantly from the Serbian Chetnik Movement and the White Eagles associated with Vojislav Šešelj and the Serbian Guard led by Vuk Drašković

descriptions: D-Block is a splinter group from Ghetto Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: D-Block (Deptford)

Unione Corse.description: The Unione Corse is a term for Corsican organized crime from the 1930s–1970s, primarily involved in the French Connection heroin trade between Turkey, France, and the United States. Based in Marseille and Corsica, it consisted of Corsican and Italian-French clans that cooperated and competed. The syndicate was a key supplier of heroin to the American market but remained subordinate to the Italian-American Mafia.

descriptions: Unione Corse is a component of the Corsican mafia managing modern criminal activity.
edges.evidence_quote: modern (1990s–present) criminal activity is managed by the Marseille-based Unione Corse and Northern Corsica–based Gang de la Brise de Mer

descriptions: Guérini clan ruled Unione Corse after Carbone's death.

descriptions: East Coast Crips killed a Grape Street Watts Crips member, sparking a war between the two sets.
edges.evidence_quote: Brandon 'BL' Bullard, a veteran of the Grape Street Watts Crips, was murdered by the East Coast Crips in 2008. The murder of Bullard sparked a war that resulted in 26 wounded and 9 dead in the six weeks that followed.

descriptions: East Coast Crips and Hoover Crips severed their alliance.

descriptions: Mexican Mafia put a green light (authorization to kill) on East Coast Crips.
edges.evidence_quote: This resulted in the Mexican Mafia putting a “green light” out on the East Coast Crips.

descriptions: Al-Ahed is a newspaper associated with Hezbollah.
edges.evidence_quote: Hezbollah is also associated with Al-Ahed ("the Covenant"), a weekly newspaper and online platform that publishes news reports, editorials, and ideological material aligned with the organisation's positions.

descriptions: Grudge Bringers killed a Bandidos president in retaliation for a fight.
edges.evidence_quote: On 14 January 2020, Shane "Wock" De Britt, president of the Bandidos' Central West chapter based in Molong , was fatally shot in the head with a 12-gauge Franchi shotgun at his home in Eurimbla in retaliation after members of the Wellington -based Grudge Bringers gang were beaten and robbed of their gang " colours " during a fight with Bandidos.

Somali Outlaws.description: The Somali Outlaws are a Somali American street gang based in the Minneapolis–Saint Paul metropolitan area, active since the 2000s. Their territory includes Cedar-Riverside, south Minneapolis, and Saint Paul, with a presence in Nashville. The gang is involved in drug trafficking and human trafficking, and its leader Mustafa Ali was killed in 2019.

descriptions: Somali Outlaws are in conflict with 1627 Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws' main rivals are Hot Boyz, Madhiban With Attitude, and 1627 Boys
